text: LAB-03 DIGITAL COMMUNICATION Name: Vivek Rakesh Tiwary Roll No.: 240102040 Subject: Digital Communication Submitted To: Bhanu Priya Ma'am Branch Electronics and Communication Engineering EXPERIMENT-03 Title of the Experiment: Design and setup a circuit for Frequency Shift Keying (FSK) using a 555 timer and transistor-based switching and to plot the corresponding FSK waveform Objectives: ० To study the operating principle of Frequency Shift Keying (FSK) as a digital modulation technique. न To implement FSK generation using a 55 timer in astable mode with transistor switching न To observe and analyse the FSK waveform using a Digital Storage Oscilloscope (DSO) Apparatus / Components Required: S.No. | Component Specification 3 | Resistors Rs = 10 छा, Ra = 50 छत, Re = 50 KO, Rb = 47 6) CEA Function Generator (Data Input) | Digital signal ~ 150 Hz BC Fores Supt Digital Storage Oscilloscope Dual channel EE Theory (Without Equations): Frequency Shift Keying (FSK) is a digital modulation techni

tion Generator (Data Input) | Digital signal ~ 150 Hz BC Fores Supt Digital Storage Oscilloscope Dual channel EE Theory (Without Equations): Frequency Shift Keying (FSK) is a digital modulation technique in which digital infor- mation is transmitted by changing the frequency of a carrier signal according to the 1 applied binary input signal In FSK modulation, the carrier switches between two discrete frequencies न Low frequency (f;) corresponding to logic LOW (binary 0) न High frequency (f2) corresponding to logic HIGH (binary 1) In this experiment, a 555 timer operating in astable mode is used to generate a square wave. A transistor is used as a switching element to change the effective resis- tance in the timing network based on the digital input signal. This change alters the charging and discharging time of the timing capacitor, thereby shifting the output fre- quency between two values. ‘Working Mechanism: © The digital data signal is applied to the transistor base through resisto

nd discharging time of the timing capacitor, thereby shifting the output fre- quency between two values. ‘Working Mechanism: © The digital data signal is applied to the transistor base through resistor R, © The transistor switches ON and OFF according to the input data. © This switching changes the effective timing resistance of the 555 astable circuit. न As a result, the output frequency shifts between two levels. Hence, the output is a frequency-modulated digital waveform representing the FSK signal Circuit Description: © The 555 timer is configured in astable mode to generate a square wave. © Resistors ही, Ry, and capacitor C determine the oscillation frequency. न The digital input (150 Hz) is applied to the transistor through resistor R, न The transistor alters the effective resistance path, producing two different output frequencies © The FSK output is obtained from pin 3 of the 555 timer. न All waveforms are monitored using a dual-channel DSO. path, producing two different output frequencies © The FSK output is obtained from pin 3 of the 555 timer. न All waveforms are monitored using a dual-channel DSO. Assemble the FSK circuit on the breadboard as per the circuit diagram. 3 2. Apply +5 V DC supply to the 555 timer circuit 3. Provide a digital input signal of approximately 150 Hz to the transistor base. 4. Switch ON the supply and verify proper circuit operation. 5. Comnect the DSO probes to observe the input data signal and FSK output wave- form 6. Observe the frequency shift corresponding to logic HIGH and logic LOW. Observations: Signal Observation Low-frequency digital waveform (150 Hz) FSK Output Frequency shifts between two levels corresponding, to input HIGH and LOW Observed Waveform: TAEARE/ANTAWS अ 4 po] ns caw ना कण Result: Frequency Shift Keying (FSK) was successfully generate

FSK Output Frequency shifts between two levels corresponding, to input HIGH and LOW Observed Waveform: TAEARE/ANTAWS अ 4 po] ns caw ना कण Result: Frequency Shift Keying (FSK) was successfully generated using a 555 timer and tran- sistor switching circuit. The waveform observed on the DSO confirmed that the output frequency changes between two distinet values according to the applied digital input signal Precautions: न Verify the correct pin configuration of the 555 timer before connections « Ensure proper grounding of the circuit and oscilloscope. न Check resistor and capacitor values before assembling the circuit न Avoid loose breadboard connections to prevent noise or unstable frequency. Applications: न Modems and data communication systems न Wireless telemetry 1 न Caller ID and low-speed data transmission न Digital radio communication Conclusion: The experiment demonstrates the successful generation of Frequency Shift Keying (FSK) using a 555 timer with transistor-based switching. T

d data transmission न Digital radio communication Conclusion: The experiment demonstrates the successful generation of Frequency Shift Keying (FSK) using a 555 timer with transistor-based switching. The output waveform verifies that the frequency shifts between two distinct values based on the input digital signal, confirm- ing the fundamental principle of FSK modulation and its suitability for reliable digital communication 5
